title: documentation: porting guide, API cheatsheet, fixtures README, spec sync
description:
Four documentation updates:
1. Write the lifecycle fixtures section in fixtures/README.md explaining the fixture format
2. Update docs/porting.md: add Step 5 — Widget Lifecycle after the existing four steps
3. Update docs/reference/api-cheatsheet.qmd with new symbols (reduce, effect types, phase enum, TransportCapabilities)
4. Sync the protocol delta spec (cycle detection + depth cap) into main openspec/specs/protocol/spec.md

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 8
acceptance_criteria: All four doc changes committed. Verify with markdown linter.

title: Julia: implement lifecycle reducer + shell, replace Widget code
description:
Port the Python lifecycle reducer and shell to Julia. Define effect/event types in Cositos.jl, implement reduce(phase, event, current_state, capabilities), certify against fixtures/lifecycle/*.json, implement WidgetShell, and replace the current Widget mutable struct (search for 'mutable struct Widget' — not line numbers, which are fragile).

Scope:
- 5.1: Define effect and event types (structs with kind symbol, or Dict)
- 5.2: Implement reduce(phase, event, current_state, capabilities) -> (new_phase, effects)
- 5.3: Certify against fixtures/lifecycle/*.json
- 5.4: Implement WidgetShell, replacing open!/send_state!/close!/_handle!/_require_open
- 5.5: Run existing host_tests.jl — all pass unchanged
- 5.6: Run e2e parity test: julia/test/runtests.jl

The existing CositosIJuliaExt transport extension remains unchanged.

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 5
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/lifecycle/spec.md
acceptance_criteria: Julia passes all lifecycle fixtures + existing host_tests.jl unchanged.

title: Python: implement imperative shell (WidgetShell) and replace Widget internals
description:
Implement the imperative WidgetShell that calls reduce, walks effects, delegates to Transport/host callbacks, handles comm-id feedback loop, and wires inbound message dispatch. Then replace the existing Widget class internals to delegate to the shell (public API unchanged).

Tasks (see openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md groups 3-4):
1. Implement WidgetShell with public API methods
2. Wire inbound callback loop (parse_message + put_buffers → Inbound events)
3. Wire comm-id feedback loop
4. Write tests/test_shell.py (parity with test_model.py)
5. Replace Widget class internals in model.py
6. Run full test suite

Design: the shell is ~20-50 lines per language. All branching logic lives in reduce, which is fixture-tested.

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/lifecycle-shell/spec.md
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md task groups 3-4
acceptance_criteria: All existing tests pass (test_model.py, test_lifecycle.py, test_shell.py, test_e2e_jupyter.py). Public API unchanged.

title: Python: implement lifecycle reducer (pure reduce function + fixtures)
description:
Define the pure lifecycle reducer for Python: effect types (Send, Listen, ApplyState, InvokeCustom, Error), event types (Open, SendState, SendCustom, Inbound, Close, CommIdAssigned), phase enum (UNOPENED/OPEN/CLOSED), TransportCapabilities dataclass, and the reduce(phase, event, current_state, capabilities) function.

Write fixture JSON files in fixtures/lifecycle/ and tests/test_lifecycle.py.

Design decisions (see openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/design.md):
- reduce is a pure function, called by the imperative shell (task 3)
- The shell calls put_buffers before feeding inbound events
- Capabilities are passed to reduce, which decides which effects to produce
- Error effects are converted to RuntimeError by the shell

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/lifecycle/spec.md
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md task group 2
acceptance_criteria: reduce() passes all lifecycle fixtures. No I/O, no transport required.

title: buffer-split: add cycle detection and depth capping across all languages
description:
Add cycle detection and depth capping to the remove_buffers/_separate algorithm in Julia, C#, Clojure, and R (Python already has it). Create code-based test cases in each language's test suite. Currently only Python's implementation detects cycles and caps nesting depth; the other four ports would infinite-loop or stack-overflow on cyclic/deep widget state.

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/protocol/spec.md
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md task group 1

Tasks:
- Add ancestors-tuple cycle detection to Julia's _separate
- Add _MAX_DEPTH capping to Julia's _separate
- Add both to C#'s Separate
- Add both to Clojure's separate
- Add both to R's separate
- Create code-based test cases (not JSON fixtures — cycles can't be represented in JSON)
- Update fixture-certification tests to assert cycle and depth errors
acceptance_criteria: All 5 language ports detect cycles and exceed-depth and raise clear errors. Test suite passes.

title: bug: Julia send_state! omits anywidget identity re-merge on full send
description: Julia send_state! (Cositos.jl lines 431-440) never calls model_identity/view_identity on a full send (include=nothing), unlike Python reference implementation. The RequestState handler dispatches to send_state!(w) with no include, so a JupyterLab reload-without-kernel-restart triggers this path. After reload, the widget never re-renders because the update reply lacks _model_name/_model_module etc. See docs/evaluations/cositos-julia-identity-remerge-bug.md
acceptance_criteria:
send_state! without include merges model_identity + view_identity into state
send_state! with explicit include skips identity merge (preserves existing behavior)
host_tests.jl has a regression test asserting identity fields present after full send_state!
close_reason: Fixed send_state! identity re-merge on full send. See 55ce62c

title: R: implement lifecycle reducer + shell (first live widget)
description:
Port the lifecycle reducer and imperative shell to R (r/core.R). This port currently has zero lifecycle code — this is the first step toward a live widget. Implement reduce, certify against fixtures/lifecycle/*.json, then build the imperative shell.

Tasks:
- Implement reduce(phase, event, current_state, capabilities) in r/core.R
- Certify R reducer against fixtures/lifecycle/*.json
- Implement R imperative shell (Widget wrapper around the reducer)

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 6 (formerly cositos-07k, split per SCOPE-001)
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/lifecycle/spec.md
acceptance_criteria: R passes lifecycle fixtures. No regressions in existing protocol certification.

title: verification: run examples and full test suite across all languages
description:
Final verification pass after all implementation is complete. Run all existing examples against the shell-based Widget to confirm no regressions.

Scope:
1. Run all existing examples: examples/benchmarks/, examples/dashboard/, examples/e2e/, examples/notebooks/, examples/parity/
2. Run full test suite across all languages: mise run verify

Blocked on: cositos-ha6 (Python shell), cositos-328 (Julia), cositos-cn1 (C#), cositos-9dj (R)

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 9
acceptance_criteria: All examples render correctly. All tests pass in all languages. mise run verify passes.

title: capability flags: generalize supports_receive per-event, document Clojure gaps
description:
Generalize the single supports_receive flag into per-event-kind flags via a TransportCapabilities struct.

Scope (note: Python TransportCapabilities struct is defined by cositos-jjp task 2.4 — import from there):
1. Define TransportCapabilities struct for Julia (mirroring Python's definition in cositos-jjp task 2.4)
2. Update CommTransport (src/cositos/jupyter.py): replace 'supports_receive: bool = True' class attribute with a 'capabilities: TransportCapabilities = TransportCapabilities()' instance attribute. Keep supports_receive as a property for backward compat.
3. Update IJuliaCommTransport (julia/ext/CositosIJuliaExt.jl): same pattern as Python
4. Add a docstring to Clojure's clojupyter_transport.clj listing which capability flags are False (no buffers, no custom, no request_state)

Not in scope: docs/porting.md clojupyter-section update (that's in cositos-m6q).

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/design.md D5
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 7
acceptance_criteria: TransportCapabilities struct exists in Python and Julia. Clojure transport docstring lists which flags are False. porting.md updated.

title: C# and R: implement lifecycle reducer + shell (first live widget)
description:
Port the lifecycle reducer and imperative shell to C# (csharp/Core.cs) and R (r/core.R). These two ports currently have zero lifecycle code — this is the first step toward a live widget in each. Implement reduce, certify against fixtures/lifecycle/*.json, then build the imperative shell.

Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/tasks.md group 6
Ref: openspec/changes/widget-lifecycle-reducer/specs/lifecycle/spec.md
acceptance_criteria: C# and R pass lifecycle fixtures. No regressions in existing protocol certification.
close_reason: split into separate C# and R tickets per issue-review SCOPE-001

title: Pre-requisite: add git remote + GitHub Pages + secrets before CI fires
description:
The CI/docs/release workflows are wired and ready, but won't fire until:
1. A GitHub remote is added (git remote add origin git@github.com:<org>/cositos.git)
2. GitHub Pages is enabled in the repo Settings (Source: GitHub Actions)
3. For releases: NPM_TOKEN and PYPI_API_TOKEN secrets are set in the repo
4. _quarto.yml site-url is uncommented with the real URL
close_reason: Remote added (git@github.com:sashakile/cositos.git), site-url set, workflows created

title: Create release workflow (PyPI + npm + GitHub Release)
description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml triggered by git tag v*. Publishes cositos to PyPI, @cositos/front to npm, and creates a GitHub Release.
